title: From Privacy to Trust in the Agentic Era: A Taxonomy of Challenges in Trustworthy Federated Learning Through the Lens of Trust Report 2.0
description_es: Este artículo presenta una taxonomía de desafíos en el aprendizaje federado confiable, examinando los problemas de privacidad y confianza en el contexto de la era de los agentes de IA y el Trust Report 2.0.
abstract: As we enter the agentic era of AI, federated learning faces new challenges in balancing privacy and trust. This paper presents a comprehensive taxonomy of challenges in trustworthy federated learning, examined through the lens of Trust Report 2.0. We identify key privacy-preserving mechanisms, trust calibration requirements, and governance frameworks necessary for deploying federated learning systems in sensitive domains.

citations.apa: Rodríguez-Barroso, N., García-Márquez, M., Luzón, M. V., & Herrera, F. (2026). From Privacy to Trust in the Agentic Era: A Taxonomy of Challenges in Trustworthy Federated Learning Through the Lens of Trust Report 2.0. Information Fusion, 132, 104236.
citations.mla: Rodríguez-Barroso, Nuria, Mario García-Márquez, María Victoria Luzón, and Francisco Herrera. "From Privacy to Trust in the Agentic Era: A Taxonomy of Challenges in Trustworthy Federated Learning Through the Lens of Trust Report 2.0." Information Fusion, vol. 132, 2026, p. 104236.
